title: NOC 41300 – Social Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Social Workers under NOC 41300 are licensed professionals who support individuals, families, and communities in overcoming social, emotional, and economic challenges. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is essential within Canada’s healthcare, education, correctional, and community support systems. Social Workers play a vital role in child protection, mental health services, crisis intervention, rehabilitation, and social policy development across Canada’s public and private sectors.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group413 – Professional occupations in social and community servicesMinor Group4130 – Social and community service professionalsNOC Code41300

duties: Social Workers perform some or all of the following duties:
Interview clients individually, in families, or groups
Refer clients to financial, legal, housing, and healthcare services
Investigate cases of child abuse or neglect
Advocate for client groups within communities
Conduct social research and assist in community development
Provide mediation services and psychosocial assessments
Supervise other social workers (in senior roles)
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Social Work (BSW) required in most provinces
In Alberta, a bachelor’s degree or diploma in social work required
Provincial written and/or oral examinations may be required
Registration with provincial governing body mandatory in:Newfoundland and LabradorNova ScotiaPrince Edward IslandNew BrunswickQuebecOntarioAlbertaBritish Columbia
Use of titles “Social Worker” and “Registered Social Worker” (RSW) is regulated in all provinces
Membership in provincial association usually required
Foreign-trained professionals must complete credential assessment and provincial registration before practicing.
salary_prospects: $46,200 – $91,400 per year, depending on experience, specialization, and province.
Higher earnings are possible in supervisory or northern community roles.
job_outlook: The outlook for Social Workers remains positive and stable due to:
Northern territories and rural communities show particularly strong demand.
labour_market_demand: Strong demand exists across Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, and Northern territories.
Career progression to supervisory and policy roles
Social Workers are recognized as regulated professionals, providing structured and secure career pathways.
pathways: Healthcare and social services targeted streams
Provincial registration must be completed before professional practice.
